name: Customer prints public quote to PDF for their records
description: Asha receives the quote email, opens the public-quote page, and uses Cmd+P / browser 'Print to PDF' to save it. The print stylesheet must hide non-essential UI (the accept/decline action bar, the small 'powered by' chrome at the bottom is OK to keep), preserve the header (contractor + business + contact), keep the line-items table legible, and break-page sensibly between the table and the totals.

persona.name: Asha Patel
persona.context: Wants to file the quote in her renovation folder before deciding.

preconditions: Public quote URL is reachable; quote is in 'sent' status (not yet decided)

steps.expect: Page renders normally — accept/decline bar visible, header card visible
Print preview hides the accept/decline action bar (e.g. via @media print { .qpub__cta-row { display: none } })
Body background is white; inks are dark; line-items table has visible borders for printed legibility

steps.action: Saves as PDF and re-opens it
steps.expect: PDF includes contractor identity, scope sentence, full line items, and the cents-correct total; QR/URL footer optional

evaluationCriteria.check: Printed page reads as a clean document — typography survives the loss of color
Customer feels they have a real record without screenshotting
Page-break controls keep totals on the same page as the last line item when possible
Tagged PDF (where supported) preserves the table semantics
